summary: Matched a large additional portion of sysdolphin baselib HSD_LObj by moving substantial code from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.s into src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c, updating lobj.h layout/types, and propagating newly identified HSD_LObj symbol names across remaining assembly callsites. The PR was explicitly described by the author as not finished but still shiftable, so it prioritized a stable matching checkpoint over a complete LObj decompilation.
what_changed: Replaced a large portion of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.s with C in src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c; the file stats show 1656 assembly lines removed and 1071 C lines added.

Added or exposed LObj setup/light helper code in C, including functions visible in the diff excerpt such as HSD_LObjSetupSpecularInit, setup_spec_lightobj, setup_point_lightobj, setup_spot_lightobj, and an asm wrapper or still-unmatched HSD_LObjSetupInit.

Renamed many previously anonymous LObj call targets in assembly to HSD_LObj-prefixed symbols, including HSD_LObjDeleteCurrentAll, HSD_LObjSetupInit, HSD_LObjSetPosition, HSD_LObjSetCurrentAll, HSD_LObjAddCurrent, HSD_LObjSetInterest, HSD_LObjLoadDesc, and HSD_LObjAddAnim.

Updated lobj.h struct field typing around HSD_LightPoint/HSD_LightSpot-style attenuation and function fields, changing several small integer fields such as point_func and dist_func from u8 to u32 in the excerpt.
Removed obsolete function declarations from include/functions.h after LObj functions gained proper names/headers.
Touched downstream assembly and C files only to reflect resolved LObj symbol names, not to change their local logic.
smart_moves: The PR preserved shiftability by landing a partial but coherent LObj matching checkpoint instead of waiting for the whole module to be finished.
New HSD_LObj names were propagated across many callsites, making later decompilation of camera, fighter, stage, menu, item/interface, and map code easier to search and reason about.

The C reconstruction factored repeated lighting behavior into helper routines for specular, point, and spot light setup, matching the conceptual GX light API boundaries visible in calls like GXInitLightColor, GXInitLightPos, GXInitLightDir, GXInitLightAttn, GXInitLightDistAttn, and GXInitLightSpot.

The work retained exact labels for constants where necessary for matching, as seen in review discussion suggesting Vec const lbl_803B94A0 and lbl_803B94AC instead of descriptive local names position and interest.
The author was explicit about uncertainty in unresolved type semantics rather than overclaiming that fields were known enums.
decomp_lessons: For baselib modules, landing a shiftable partial decomp can be valuable when it replaces large assembly chunks and resolves many cross-module symbols, even if some functions remain asm or NON_MATCHING-adjacent.
When matching depends on rodata identity, anonymous label-style names such as lbl_803B94A0 may be preferable to descriptive names until the surrounding data is fully understood.
Struct field widths should follow observed layout and codegen, but semantic names like enum/pointer/unsigned should remain conservative until points of use are decompiled.
HSD lighting code often maps cleanly onto GX light setup APIs; identifying GXInitLight* call patterns can help separate point, spot, infinite, and specular light behavior.
After naming a baselib function, update surviving assembly callsites so subsequent PRs inherit semantic call names instead of func_803xxxxx placeholders.
Reviewers may challenge whether renamed asm stubs still correspond to the right function when signatures change; keep asm wrappers and symbol replacements easy to audit.
naming_conventions: Resolved LObj public functions use the HSD_LObj prefix, for example HSD_LObjDeleteCurrentAll, HSD_LObjSetupInit, HSD_LObjSetPosition, HSD_LObjSetCurrentAll, HSD_LObjAddCurrent, HSD_LObjSetInterest, HSD_LObjLoadDesc, and HSD_LObjAddAnim.
Private or local helper functions in the excerpt used lower_snake_case names such as setup_spec_lightobj, setup_point_lightobj, and setup_spot_lightobj.
Matching-sensitive constants and extern strings may retain lbl_XXXXXXXX names rather than descriptive names when exact symbol identity matters.
LOObj type constants in switches appear as LOBJ_POINT, LOBJ_SPOT, and LOBJ_INFINITE where known, but some cases still used raw numeric values in the excerpt, indicating incomplete naming certainty.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved matched code from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.s into src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c while leaving remaining unmatched assembly where needed.
Used exact global/static label names for data such as lbl_803B94A0, lbl_803B94AC, lbl_804D5D18, lbl_804D5D20, and lbl_804D5D24 to preserve rodata/sdata matching.
Kept an asm HSD_LObjSetupInit body in lobj.c after adding surrounding C helpers, indicating a hybrid decomp strategy around difficult setup code.
Propagated symbol renames in assembly without altering instruction bytes, e.g. changing bl func_80366654 comments/targets to bl HSD_LObjDeleteCurrentAll and func_80366CE8 to HSD_LObjSetPosition.
Matched light setup behavior by reproducing vector math and matrix transform calls such as PSVECNormalize, PSVECSubtract, PSVECAdd, PSMTXMUltiVec, and PSMTXMultVecSR around GX light initialization.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested retaining label-style names for zero Vec constants: Vec const lbl_803B94A0 and Vec const lbl_803B94AC instead of descriptive names position and interest.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether changing the asm function from HSD_LObjSetup(HSD_LObj*, GXColor, f32, u32) to HSD_LObjSetupInit() was a mistake.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Responded with uncertainty to the concern about the HSD_LObjSetup/HSD_LObjSetupInit asm change.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned whether fields changed to u32 were enums, pointers, or unsigned constants.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Said the point of use had not been decompiled yet, so the exact semantics were not known.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Floated the idea of typedef s32 UnkEnum in types.h for unknown enum-like values, then noted that hiding the type behind an alias might add indirection for little gain because enum certainty was lacking.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs that finish or rename remaining HSD_LObjSetup/HSD_LObjSetupInit assembly to verify whether the review concern was resolved.
Search for subsequent changes to HSD_LightPoint and related lobj.h fields to see whether point_func, spot_func, and dist_func became GX enum types or stayed integer fields.
Look for later replacement of lbl_803B94A0/lbl_803B94AC with semantic names once rodata and usage were better understood.
Search for later decompilation of callsites updated in this PR, especially grpushon, grshrineroute, grzebesroute, mncharsel, mnstagesel, lbunknown_003, mplib, and ft_unknown_006.
